author: The Third Ecumenical Council: The Council of Ephesus
book: The XII. Anathematisms of St. Cyril Against Nestorius.

classification.cautionReason: This work discusses heresy in order to refute it; do not classify it as heretical merely from its title.

lineage: The Third Ecumenical Council: The Council of Ephesus.
The XII. Anathematisms of St. Cyril Against Nestorius.

metadata.author: The Third Ecumenical Council: The Council of Ephesus

title: Anathematism XII.

verses.book: The XII. Anathematisms of St. Cyril Against Nestorius.
verses.text: Whosoever shall not recognize that the Word of God suffered in the flesh, that he was crucified in the flesh, and that likewise in that same flesh he tasted death and that he is become the first-begotten of the dead, for, as he is God, he is the life and it is he that giveth life: let him be anathema.

verses.book: The XII. Anathematisms of St. Cyril Against Nestorius.
verses.text: If any one, in confessing the sufferings of the flesh, ascribes these also to the Word of God as to the flesh in which he appeared, and thus does not distinguish the dignity of the natures; let him be anathema.

verses.book: The XII. Anathematisms of St. Cyril Against Nestorius.
The XII. Anathematisms of St. Cyril Against Nestorius.
The XII. Anathematisms of St. Cyril Against Nestorius.
verses.text: For if the body is of another, to him also must the sufferings be ascribed.

verses.book: The XII. Anathematisms of St. Cyril Against Nestorius.
verses.text: But if the flesh is the Word’s (for “The Word was made flesh”) it is necessary that the sufferings of the flesh be called his also whose is the flesh.

verses.book: The XII. Anathematisms of St. Cyril Against Nestorius.
verses.text: But whose are the sufferings, such especially as condemnation, flagellation, thirst, the cross, death, and other such like infirmities of the body, his also is the merit and the grace.

verses.book: The XII. Anathematisms of St. Cyril Against Nestorius.
verses.text: Therefore rightly and properly to none other are these sufferings attributed than to the Lord, as also the grace is from him; and we shall not be guilty of idolatry, but be the true worshippers of God, for we invoke him who is no creature nor any common man, but the natural and true Son of God, made man, and yet the same Lord and God and Saviour.

verses.book: The XII. Anathematisms of St. Cyril Against Nestorius.
verses.text: As I think, these quotations will suffice to the learned for the proof of the propositions advanced, the Divine Law plainly saying that “In the mouth of two or three witnesses every word shall be established.”

verses.book: The XII. Anathematisms of St. Cyril Against Nestorius.
verses.text: But if after this any one would still seem to be contentious, we would say to him: “Go thine own way.

verses.book: The XII. Anathematisms of St. Cyril Against Nestorius.
verses.text: We however shall follow the divine Scriptures and the faith of the Holy Fathers.”

verses.book: The XII. Anathematisms of St. Cyril Against Nestorius.
verses.text: The student should read at full length all Cyril’s defence of his anathematisms, also his answers to the criticisms of Theodoret, and to those of the Orientals, all of which will be found in his works, and in Labbe and Cossart, Concilia, Tom.

verses.book: The XII. Anathematisms of St. Cyril Against Nestorius.
